sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: The following degree requirements are required as of Winter 2025 quarter. requirementCueLines: Major requirements
Degree Requirements

The following degree requirements are required as of Winter 2025 quarter. Students who entered the American & Ethnic Studies (AES) major prior to Winter 2025 have different degree requirements. Please see your Academic Advisor for questions and academic planning.
Interdisciplinary Writing Seminar: BIS 290 (5 credits) – Prerequisite of B WRIT 135 or ENGL 141 *
AES Core Course : BISAES 305 Power, Dissent, and American Culture (5 credits)
BIS 312 Approaches to Social Research OR BIS 340 Approaches to Cultural Research (5 credits) – minimum 2.0 GPA required
AES Courses (30 credits) – from the approved subject areas below
Historical and Social Inquiry course (5 credits)
Textual Analysis and Interpretation course (5 credits)
Critical Theory and Practice course (5 credits)
Additional AES courses (15 credits) – choose from Historical and Social Inquiry course list, Textual Analysis and Interpretation list, or Critical Theory and Practice list
Additional School of IAS Coursework (20 credits)
10 credits of Composition Coursework
B WRIT 133 or B WRIT 134 or ENGL 131 or equivalent (5 credits)
B WRIT 135 or ENGL 141 or equivalent (5 credits)
TOTAL = 75 Credits
Note: Classes in this option are offered primarily during daytime hours.
Additional School of IAS Requirements
Residency Requirement: 30 credits must be completed in residency at UW Bothell.

Interdisciplinary Practices & Reflection (IPR): The IPR requirement can overlap with the 75 credits of major coursework, or it can be completed through elective credits. Please see the IPR webpage for course options.
AES majors are encouraged to register for BISAES 465 Advanced Seminar in American Ethnic Studies to complete their IPR requirement.

Upper Division Credit Policy: A maximum of 35 credits earned in 100- and 200-level courses may apply toward the major. Remaining credits must be earned in upper division courses (300-level and above).
BISAES 305 Power, Dissent, and American Culture
BIS 312 Approaches to Social Research (min grade of 2.0)
BIS 340 Approaches to Cultural Research (min grade of 2.0)
chooseStatements: Additional AES courses (15 credits) – choose from Historical and Social Inquiry course list, Textual Analysis and Interpretation list, or Critical Theory and Practice list
